summary: Frontend testing of Espace Agenda V3 iteration 6: Blog.jsx & BlogPost.jsx refactor. All 15 feature requirements verified. dangerouslySetInnerHTML implementation is CORRECT (no hardcoded text). One notable issue: DB content is stored as Markdown (not HTML), causing raw `#` characters to render visibly in the browser. prose-blog CSS styles exist and are correctly defined but won't apply to Markdown-formatted content.

minor.issue: API returns only 3 posts despite 9 seed articles. Either the default limit is 3 or only 3 are published. Worth confirming pagination/limit behavior.

ui_bugs.issue: VISUAL BUG: Blog post content is stored in DB as Markdown (# Introduction, ## Les enjeux...). dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders raw markdown text — visible `#` and `##` symbols appear on screen. The prose-blog h2/h3 CSS styles exist but won't apply since there are no actual <h2>/<h3> HTML elements in the rendered output. Fix: either store HTML in DB, or apply a Markdown parser (e.g. marked.js) before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

issues: ACCEPTABLE (not a bug in Blog.jsx): input[type='email'] found comes from Footer component (x-file-name='Footer'), not from Blog.jsx. Blog.jsx itself has no newsletter section. Requirement is satisfied.

action_items: CRITICAL: Blog post content is stored as Markdown in DB but dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders it as raw HTML. Either: (1) re-seed blog posts with HTML content, or (2) add a Markdown→HTML conversion using `marked` or `react-markdown` before passing to dangerouslySetInnerHTML. This causes visible `#` symbols on the article content area.
critical_code_review_comments: BlogPost.jsx dangerouslySetInnerHTML fix is correctly implemented — no hardcoded text remains in JSX. Content comes from post.content via the API.

index.css prose-blog styles verified: h2/h3 use Cormorant Garamond, p uses #5E6C60 color, blockquote has green (#5A7161) left border. Styles are correct but won't render since DB content is Markdown not HTML.
Blog.jsx empty state (posts.length === 0) correctly shows 'Les articles arrivent bientôt' per code review — data-testid='blog-empty-contact-cta' present.
No bg-muted class found in Blog.jsx page DOM (only shadcn internals if any — none found).
Both blog-final-cta and blogpost-final-cta CTAs display 'Planifier un échange' from globalCTA.primary — correct.

success_rate.frontend: 93% (14/15 visible tests pass; 1 visual bug: Markdown content rendered as raw text)

test_credentials: Admin: /admin/login, password JQi]=3+8Azc4 (not needed for this run)
seed_data_creation: None. 9 seed articles already in DB, /api/blog/posts returns 3.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Blog.jsx and BlogPost.jsx structural requirements pass. Key remaining issue: blog post content (post.content) is stored as Markdown in MongoDB but rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML which expects HTML. The page shows raw `# Introduction` etc. with visible hash symbols. Fix is either: re-seeding DB articles with HTML content, or adding a Markdown parser. data-testids all present: blog-post-card-{id}, blog-read-link-{id}, blog-final-cta, blog-empty-contact-cta, blogpost-inline-cta, blogpost-final-cta, related-post-{id}.

rca_of_issue.issue: Markdown content rendered as raw text in BlogPost
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Seed data in content.js stores blog post content as Markdown strings (with # for headings). BlogPost.jsx uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML which injects the string as raw HTML. Since Markdown is not HTML, the browser renders the literal `#` characters as visible text instead of headings. The prose-blog CSS classes (.prose-blog h2, .prose-blog h3) are correctly defined but never applied because no actual <h2>/<h3> elements exist in the rendered DOM.
rca_of_issue.reproduction: Navigate to /blog/1, scroll to content area — visible '# Introduction ## Les enjeux...' text with hash symbols
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Option 1 (recommended): Update seed data / admin panel to save content as HTML. Option 2: In BlogPost.jsx, use a Markdown parser (e.g. `marked.parse(post.content)`) and pass result to dangerouslySetInnerHTML: `dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: marked.parse(post.content || '') }}`
